page: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The processor features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for matrix operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8i.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance in specific computational tasks. Single-core Geekbench scores reach top-tier levels, particularly in image processing and text processing workloads. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 and single-threaded gzip compression both register in the strong tier. For machine learning tasks, the instance achieves strong prompt processing speeds on small and medium LLM models, alongside top-tier text generation speeds on small models. However, its stress-ng CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks remain in the average tier, indicating a balance between raw compute throughput and memory access speeds.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring ultra-low latency disk access, though the high-speed DDR5 memory helps mitigate data transfer bottlenecks within memory-bound tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.8xlarge offers a dense compute profile with dedicated CPU allocation, making it suitable for compute-heavy workloads without the overhead of bundled local storage. It is positioned for tasks such as software compilation, image processing, text processing, static web serving, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, offering a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. While it lacks local storage, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in image and text processing, alongside strong results in single-threaded compression and small-model LLM inference. Conversely, its multi-core stress-ng and memory bandwidth benchmarks sit in the average tier. This instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for compilation and text processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for intensive computational workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor
64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage included
Top-tier performance in image processing, text processing, and small-model LLM inference
